IF THAT RULING DETERMINES THAT PLAINTIFFS, OR ANY OF THEM, HAVE STANDING, THEN ANY DISCOVERY NOW PENDING IS DEEMED TO HAVE BEEN SERVED AND DEFENDANTS SHALL HAVE THIRTY DAYS TO RESPOND UNLESS MUTUALLY AGREED OTHERWISE. NOTHING IN THE INSTANT DISCOVERY RULING SHALL BE USED BY EITHER PARTY AS EVIDENCE OF A SUBSTANTIVE ANALYSIS BY THIS COURT OF THE ISSUES TO BE DECIDED IN THE DEMURRERS. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JOI SCHEELE, TALK FREE, INC. AND THE SCHEELE TRUSTS JOINT MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST DAVID RAWLINGS IS GRANTED ON THE FOLLOWING CONDITIONS: 1) THE CROSS-COMPLAINT MUST BE SERVED BY JULY 24, 2017; 2) MOVING PARTIES MUST SERVE VERIFIED CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES NO LATER THAN AUGUST 21, 2017 TO THE FOLLOWING DEEM-SERVED DISCOVERY: A) STATE ALL FACTS IN SUPPORT OF BOTH CLAIMS IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, B) IDENTIFY AND PROVIDE FULL CONTACT INFORMATION FOR EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY OF THOSE FACTS AND STATE THE FACTS KNOWN TO EACH PERSON, AND C) IDENTIFY AND PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS WHICH REFER TO OR REFLECT ALL SUCH FACTS; THE PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS COMPULSORY AND MAY ONLY BE DISALLOWED IF FOUND TO HAVE BEEN FILED IN BAD FAITH. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT IT IS FILED IN BAD FAITH. HOWEVER, DEFENDANTS' DECISION TO FILE THIS MOTION ON JUNE 15 TO BE HEARD ON JULY 21 WHEN THE TRIAL DATE IS SEPTEMBER 11 AND THEY KNEW THE BASES FOR THE MOTION AND COULD FILE IT ON MAY 16 SHOWS A CAVALIER ATTITUDE TOWARD MR. RAWLINGS' OBVIOUS DESIRE NOT TO DELAY THE TRIAL. DEFENDANTS SHOULD HAVE FILED THEIR MOTION EARLIER AND SOUGHT TO DO SO ON SHORTENED TIME. THE CONDITIONS IMPOSED ON THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION ARE DONE TO RECTIFY DEFENDANTS' APPARENT INDIFFERENCE TO PLACING MR. RAWLINGS IN A POSITION WHERE HE WOULD BE FORCED TO SEEK A TRIAL CONTINUANCE OR FOREGO PROCEDURAL RIGHTS. TRIAL DATE REMAINS THE SAME. MR. BYRNE TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER AND TO SEND IT BY WAY OF E-MAIL. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

IS A DELAWARE CORPORATION. PURSUANT TO THE INTERNAL AFFAIRS DOCTRINE, THIS DISPUTE IS GOVERNED BY DELAWARE LAW. (SEE CAL. CORP. CODE SEC. 2116; SEE ALSO VAUGHN V. LJ INTERN, INC. Off Calendar Oct-23-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Maureen McFadden, Esq. (510-835-5203) for Plaintiff DAVID RAWLINGS.
Paul Byrne, Esq. (415-357-2094) for Defendant.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Plaintiff reports matter is uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT DAVID RAWLINGS' DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant Rawlings' demurrer to the second cause of action for unjust enrichment in the cross-complaint filed by Talk Free, Inc. and Joi Scheele is overruled as to Talk Free and sustained without leave to amend as to Ms. Scheele, both individually and as a trustee. While unjust enrichment is not itself a cause of action, liberally construing the second cause of action it alleges a viable claim for an implied contract (quasi-contract) requiring Mr. Rawlings to follow the PTO cap and comply with the other matters referenced in paragraph 37 of the cross-complaint and seeks restitution for Mr. Rawlings' breach of that implied contract. While Talk Free is a party to that implied contract and thus has "standing" to sue for its breach, Ms. Scheele does not, even though she is a substantial creditor of Talk Free.

Counsel for Mr. Rawlings to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Off Calendar Aug-25-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS JOI SCHEELE, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND AS TRUSTEE , STAN TEETS, AN INDIVIDUAL, DOUG COLE, AN INDIVIDUAL, JOI SCHEELE, Talk Free's Joint Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings

Defendants Joi Scheele, Stan Teets, Doug Cole and Talk Free, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the first amended complaint is denied in its entirety. The sole basis for the motion as to the first, second and third causes of action is the assertion that the challenged actions were the product of defendants' valid exercise of business judgment. This argument was previously made in the demurrer filed by the individual defendants (see table of contents of memorandum filed April 10, 2015) and rejected by Judge Goldsmith on September 22, 2015. Defendants have not shown that as a matter of law that the fourth cause of action is inapplicable as to the Scheele Trust or unnecessary as to Talk Free. The sole basis for the motion as to the fifth cause of action is the assertion that Rawlings had not existing relationship with Genband. While this precise argument does not appear to have been raised on the demurrers, there is no persuasive reason offered why it was not and the argument was implicitly rejected by Judge Goldsmith on September 22, 2015 when he stated that there was sufficient allegation of wrongful conduct to support the fifth cause of action. Even if meritorious, which it is not, defendants' eleventh hour motion would at most result in granting leave to plaintiffs to file a second amended complaint, which would only serve to delay the trial of a case that is quite aged already. Doing so is not in the interests of justice. Order signed. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. RULING - The matter was called twice and in the afternoon for a case management conference. The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: DEFENDANTS JOI SCHEELE, TALK FREE, INC. and the Scheele Trusts Joint Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint Against David Rawlings is GRANTED on the following conditions: 1) the cross-complaint must be served by July 24, 2017; 2) moving parties must serve verified code-compliant responses no later than August 21, 2017 to the following deem-served discovery: a) state all facts in support of both claims in the cross-complaint, b) identify and provide full contact information for each person with knowledge of any of those facts and state the facts known to each person, and c) identify and produce all documents which refer to or reflect all such facts; The proposed cross-complaint is compulsory and may only be disallowed if found to have been filed in bad faith. The court does not find that it is filed in bad faith. However, defendants' decision to file this motion on June 15 to be heard on July 21 when the trial date is September 11 and they knew the bases for the motion and could file it on May 16 shows a cavalier attitude toward Mr. Rawlings' obvious desire not to delay the trial. Defendants should have filed their motion earlier and sought to do so on shortened time. The conditions imposed on the granting of the motion are done to rectify defendants' apparent indifference to placing Mr. Rawlings in a position where he would be forced to seek a trial continuance or forego procedural rights. Trial date remains the same. Mr. Byrne to prepare form of order and to send it by way of e-mail. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH S. LEVENTHAL OF THE LEVENTHAL LAW, TEL. NO. (619) 356-3518 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. ERNEST BANCHERO, TEL. NO. 398-7000 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS DOUG COLE, JOI SCHEELE, STAN TEETS, AND TALK FREE, INC.'S Motion To Require Plaintiffs To Post Bond In The Amount Of $50,00 is Denied. Talk Free, Inc. is a Delaware corporation. Pursuant to the Internal Affairs Doctrine, this dispute is governed by Delaware law. (See Cal. Corp. Code sec. 2116; see also Vaughn v. LJ Intern, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 213, 223.) The requirement of a plaintiff posting a bond in a derivative suit is substantive, not merely procedural. (Koster v. Warren (9th Cir. 1961) 297 F.2d 418, 419-20.) Talk Free, Inc. has not cited a Delaware statute that provides for a plaintiff in a derivative suit posting a bond. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H.
